# Checklist — ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Conformance (document / requirement-set level)

> Reference checklist derived from **ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018** — focused on the **document** and **requirement-set** level (use [checklist-requirement-writing.md](checklist-requirement-writing.md) for the individual-requirement audit).
> **Ordered by severity of a *missing* check** (Critical → High → Medium → Low). Severity scale:
>
> - **Critical** — blocks any conformance claim (full or partial). Fixing is required before sign-off.
> - **High** — substantial gap; conformance claim becomes hard to defend.
> - **Medium** — quality / auditability gap; reduces value of the specification.
> - **Low** — hygiene / good practice.

## Checklist

| ID | Check name | ISO reference | Severity | What must be verified | Concrete example (✅ success / ❌ failure) |
|----|------------|---------------|----------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| IC-001 | Conformance statement declared | §4.1–4.5 | Critical | The document explicitly declares the conformance posture (Full / process only / information-item only / tailored), citing the standard. Tailored conformance lists the tailored clauses (Annex C). | ✅ "This document claims **full conformance** to ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018." ❌ Document silently uses ISO outline; no statement; auditor cannot confirm scope. |
| IC-002 | Correct information item type selected (BRS / StRS / SyRS / SRS) | §7 | Critical | The document declares which of the four normative information items it is (or combination). Content matches the information item's purpose. | ✅ Title page: "Software Requirements Specification per ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 §9.6." ❌ Document titled "SRS" but content is exclusively business goals — should have been a BRS. |
| IC-003 | All required information items produced for the project | §7 | Critical | The project produces a BRS, StRS, SyRS, and (if 12207 is adhered to) an SRS — or documents which are merged / contained in others, with reference. | ✅ Project asset map: "BRS+StRS combined in DOC-100; SyRS in DOC-110; SRS in DOC-120." ❌ Only the SRS exists; business / stakeholder rationale untraceable; auditor flags incomplete artefact set. |
| IC-004 | Requirement set is **complete** (no TBD/TBS/TBR at baseline) | §5.2.6 | Critical | At baseline / sign-off, the set contains **no TBD/TBS/TBR clauses**; iterative TBx during evolution is acceptable, with risk-aligned timeframe. | ✅ Final baseline diff shows 0 TBx. ❌ Spec baselined with 14 "TBD"; team builds anyway; rework explodes when answers arrive. |
| IC-005 | Requirement set is **consistent** | §5.2.6 | Critical | Requirements unique; do not conflict / overlap; **units and measurement systems homogeneous**; terminology consistent throughout. | ✅ Lint pass: no duplicates, units check OK (all latencies in ms, all distances in m). ❌ Two refund-window requirements (14 vs 60 days); km in one section, miles in another. |
| IC-006 | Requirement set is **feasible** | §5.2.6 | Critical | The complete set is realisable within entity constraints (cost, schedule, technical) with acceptable risk. Includes "affordable". | ✅ Estimation shows the prioritised set fits the 6-month budget with 15% buffer. ❌ p95 ≤ 50 ms + 99.999% availability + single region + $20k cloud budget — mutually impossible. |
| IC-007 | Requirement set is **able to be validated** | §5.2.6 | Critical | Satisfying the requirement set demonstrably meets entity needs within constraints (incl. legal / regulatory compliance). | ✅ Each business goal has at least one requirement that, when met, demonstrably advances it; traceability matrix shows full coverage. ❌ Many requirements; none of them mapped to the business goal or to a regulator's obligation. |
| IC-008 | Every requirement uses mandatory verbs correctly | §5.2.4 | Critical | Mandatory binding statements use **shall**. Non-requirements use **will / should / may / are / is / was**. **No "must"**; **no "shall not"** (use positive); **no "shall be able to"**; **no passive voice**. | ✅ Spec lint reports 0 occurrences of "must", "shall not", "shall be able to". ❌ Mixed verbs throughout; "the system must be fast"; "the system shall not crash" — auditor flags non-conformance. |
| IC-009 | Every requirement is **uniquely identified** with an immutable, never-reused ID | §5.2.8.2 | Critical | Each requirement has a unique identifier; identifiers are **never changed** when content changes and **never reused** after deletion. | ✅ ID scheme `REQ-FR-0001..` in a tool that enforces immutability and recycling rules. ❌ Spreadsheet where deleted rows free up IDs that get reassigned; broken traceability. |
| IC-010 | General content (front matter) present | §9.2 | Critical | Document has **title + revision notice**, **table of contents**, **list of figures**, **list of tables**, **definitions**, **references** (Compliance + Guidance subdivisions), **acronyms and abbreviations**. | ✅ Document scaffold present with all five blocks populated. ❌ No revision notice; auditor cannot confirm which version is current. |
| IC-011 | Required information item sections present per Clause 9 | §9.3 / §9.4 / §9.5 / §9.6 | High | The document includes all normative sections required by Clause 9 for the chosen information item (BRS §9.3, StRS §9.4, SyRS §9.5, SRS §9.6). Sections may be reordered per project policy. | ✅ SRS has §9.6.2..§9.6.20 sections all present. ❌ SRS missing 9.6.9 Apportioning and 9.6.18 Software system attributes. |
| IC-012 | Requirements attributes populated | §5.2.8 | High | Each requirement has attributes: **Identification, Version, Owner, Stakeholder Priority, Risk, Rationale, Difficulty, Type**. Not all may apply, but the project chooses an attribute set explicitly. | ✅ Every requirement row has the 8 attributes; tool enforces required fields. ❌ Only ID and text; no rationale, no owner; nobody can answer "why" 6 months later. |
| IC-013 | Performance / NFR requirements are quantified | §9.5.7, §9.6.14; §5.2.7 | High | Performance and other non-functional requirements are **stated in measurable terms** with units, percentile, and conditions — not vague qualifiers. | ✅ "95 % of transactions shall be processed in less than 1 s." ❌ "An operator shall not have to wait for the transaction to complete." |
| IC-014 | Interfaces fully described | §9.5.8, §9.6.11 | High | Each external interface defines: name, purpose, source/destination, **valid range / accuracy / tolerance**, **units of measure**, **timing**, relationships to other I/Os, **data formats**, **command formats**, data items. | ✅ OpenAPI 3.1 doc + interface table with all 10 fields per endpoint. ❌ "POST /orders — see code." |
| IC-015 | Verification approach specified per requirement family | §9.5.18, §9.6.19 | High | The document provides verification approaches and methods, **given in a parallel manner with the requirement information elements** (per-family verification). | ✅ Verification matrix: each requirement family (9.6.10..9.6.18) has its V&V approach (Inspection / Analysis / Demonstration / Test). ❌ Single boilerplate "tests will verify everything" — auditor flags. |
| IC-016 | Cross-references to higher-level specifications maintained | §9.6.3 (d), §9.6.10 (b) | High | The SRS is **consistent with similar statements in higher-level specifications** (SyRS / BRS); requirements are cross-referenced to earlier versions or related documents. | ✅ Each SRS requirement links to its SyRS source. ❌ SRS contradicts the SyRS on user classes; nobody noticed; build follows SRS; integration fails. |
| IC-017 | Assumptions documented and validated | §5.2.7; §9.5.19; §9.6.8 | High | All assumptions are documented (in a requirement attribute or accompanying document) and validated. Definitions are declarative statements, not requirements. | ✅ Assumptions register reviewed each release; status field tracks validation. ❌ Assumptions buried in code comments; first invalidation causes a multi-sprint refactor. |
| IC-018 | Apportionment to software / system elements | §9.6.9 | High | The SRS apportions requirements to software elements; multi-element or initially-undefined allocations are explicitly noted; a **cross-reference table** by function and element is provided. | ✅ Function × element matrix; "DEFERRED-to-v2" column for postponed work. ❌ No apportionment; team improvises; some requirements get implemented twice in two services. |
| IC-019 | Stakeholders (minimum set) identified per §5.2.2 | §5.2.2 | High | The minimum stakeholder set (users + acquirers, plus the developing/maintaining/operating organisation, plus regulators where applicable) is identified and listed. | ✅ BRS §9.3.5 lists 11 classes including "Regulator (GDPR)" and "Operations". ❌ Only product manager and sales contact consulted; ops & regulator never appear. |
| IC-020 | Iteration and recursion considered for system levels | §5.3 | Medium | Where the system is hierarchical, recursion of requirements / architecture / design processes is acknowledged; **derived requirements** are tagged as such. | ✅ "REQ-D-031 (derived from architecture decision ADR-007: pipes-and-filters)." ❌ Filter sub-team treats an architect's choice as an external client requirement; pushback later. |
| IC-021 | Operational concept / scenarios uniquely named, numbered, referenced | §9.3.10/11/17, §9.4.10/11/17 | Medium | Business / system processes, policies and scenarios are uniquely named and numbered, and **referenced** from the description of the business processes. | ✅ "Process BP-007 references Scenario SC-014." ❌ Inline narrative with no IDs; impossible to trace which process a requirement supports. |
| IC-022 | Quality model identified for quality requirements | §9.4.14, §9.5.6, §9.6.13/18 | Medium | The quality model used (e.g., **ISO/IEC 25010**) is named, and requirements map to its characteristics (performance, compatibility, reliability, security, maintainability, portability, usability, …). | ✅ "Quality requirements conform to ISO/IEC 25010 — see mapping in Appendix Q." ❌ Quality requirements scattered; no model named; auditor can't tell what was considered. |
| IC-023 | Compliance references include date + version + applicable parts | §5.2.7 (incomplete references) | Medium | Every referenced standard / regulation cites a **date + version**, and where applicable, restricts to the relevant parts (no "GDPR" — use "GDPR Art. 32"). | ✅ "ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A.5.x"; "GDPR Art. 32." ❌ "Must comply with ISO 27001 and GDPR." |
| IC-024 | Process requirements kept out of the specification | §8.4.1 | Medium | The specification does not embed *how-to-develop* process requirements; those belong in contract documentation (SoW). If included, they are **clearly identified** as process requirements. | ✅ Process requirements moved to the SoW; the few left are tagged `[PROCESS]`. ❌ SRS mandates a specific design method, mixing it with product requirements. |
| IC-025 | Modes and states defined where applicable | §9.5.10 | Medium | If the system operates in multiple modes / states, they are defined (with diagrams) and modes-and-states requirements are stated. | ✅ State diagram with "Normal / Degraded / Recovery" modes; requirements per mode. ❌ Modes assumed implicit; behaviour in degraded mode is undefined; auditor flags. |
| IC-026 | Environmental conditions enumerated | §9.5.12 | Medium | All applicable environmental conditions are listed: natural, induced, electromagnetic, self-induced, threat, cooperative; plus legal/regulatory, political, economic, social, business. | ✅ Environment matrix with applicability per category. ❌ "Will operate in normal conditions" — unspecified. |
| IC-027 | Information items can live in repository / model (no doc-only assumption) | §7 NOTE; §4.4 NOTE 2 | Low | The team has decided whether the information items are physical docs, repository entries, or models, and documents this choice; conformance does not require paper documentation. | ✅ "Information items maintained in modelling tool X; conformance verified via report Y." ❌ Team assumes paper docs are mandatory; effort wasted. |
| IC-028 | Definitive baseline placed under configuration management | §9.2.1; §5.2.8.2 | Low | The specification baseline is under CM; version history shows author, date, reason. | ✅ Git-tracked; signed commits; release tags. ❌ Multiple "final_v2_real_FINAL.docx" floating on a shared drive. |
| IC-029 | Supporting information explicitly scoped | §9.6.20 | Low | When an SRS includes sample I/O, cost analysis, user surveys, special packaging — the document **explicitly states** whether these are part of the requirements. | ✅ Appendix marked "Informative — NOT requirements." ❌ Sample formats taken as binding; supplier delivers exactly the sample. |
| IC-030 | Tailored conformance: tailored clauses and rationale declared | §4.5.2 | Low | If tailored conformance is claimed, the tailored clauses and their rationale are listed in an appendix per Annex C. | ✅ Appendix C-T lists tailored sub-clauses, rationale, mitigation. ❌ Conformance silently partial; auditor cannot tell what is missing or why. |
